    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 752
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/752](https://www.erdosproblems.com/752)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA question of ErdŇĎs, Faudree, and Schelp. Let $G$ be a graph with minimum degree $k$ and
    25‚Üígirth $> 2s$. Must there be $\gg k^s$ many distinct cycle lengths in $G$?
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíErdŇĎs, Faudree, and Schelp proved it when $s = 2$.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[SuVe08] Sudakov, B. and Verstra√ęte, J., *Cycle lengths in sparse graphs*. Combinatorica 28
    30‚Üí(2008), 357‚Äď372.
    31‚Üí-/
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üínamespace Erdos752
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- The set of cycle lengths occurring in a simple graph. -/
    38‚Üídef cycleLengths {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    39‚Üí  {n | ‚ąÉ (v : V) (p : G.Walk v v), p.IsCycle ‚ąß p.length = n}
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/--
    42‚Üí**Sudakov‚ÄďVerstra√ęte Theorem (ErdŇĎs Problem 752)** [SuVe08]:
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíThere exists an absolute constant $c > 0$ such that for every finite graph $G$
    45‚Üíwith minimum degree at least $k$ and girth greater than $2s$, the number of
    46‚Üídistinct cycle lengths in $G$ is at least $c \cdot k^s$.
    47‚Üí-/
    48‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    49‚Üítheorem erdos_752 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    50‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (V : Type*) [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]
    52‚Üí      (G : SimpleGraph V) [DecidableRel G.Adj]
    53‚Üí      (k s : ‚Ąē),
    54‚Üí      (‚ąÄ v : V, k ‚Č§ G.degree v) ‚Üí
    55‚Üí      (‚ąÄ (v : V) (p : G.Walk v v), p.IsCycle ‚Üí 2 * s < p.length) ‚Üí
    56‚Üí      c * (k : ‚ĄĚ) ^ s ‚Č§ ((cycleLengths G).ncard : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    57‚Üí  sorry
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üíend Erdos752
    60‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 751
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/751](https://www.erdosproblems.com/751)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph with chromatic number $\chi(G) = 4$. If $m_1 < m_2 < \cdots$ are the
    25‚Üílengths of the cycles in $G$, can $\min(m_{i+1} - m_i)$ be arbitrarily large?
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíThe answer is no: Bondy and Vince [BoVi98] proved that every graph with minimum degree
    28‚Üíat least $3$ has two cycles whose lengths differ by at most $2$, and hence the same is
    29‚Üítrue for every graph with chromatic number $4$.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[BoVi98] Bondy, J. A. and Vince, A., _Cycles in a graph whose lengths differ by one or
    32‚Üítwo_. Journal of Graph Theory (1998), 11-15.
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos751
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- The set of cycle lengths occurring in a simple graph. -/
    40‚Üídef cycleLengths {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    41‚Üí  {n | ‚ąÉ (v : V) (p : G.Walk v v), p.IsCycle ‚ąß p.length = n}
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- **ErdŇĎs Problem 751**: If $G$ is a graph with $\chi(G) = 4$ and $m_1 < m_2 < \cdots$
    44‚Üíare the cycle lengths of $G$, the minimum gap $\min(m_{i+1} - m_i)$ cannot be made
    45‚Üíarbitrarily large. Bondy and Vince [BoVi98] proved that every graph with minimum degree
    46‚Üíat least $3$ has two cycles whose lengths differ by at most $2$, which implies the
    47‚Üínegative answer since $\chi(G) = 4$ forces a subgraph with minimum degree $\geq 3$.
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí[BoVi98] -/
    50‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    51‚Üítheorem erdos_751 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    52‚Üí    (‚ąÄ C : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ (V : Type) (_ : Fintype V) (G : SimpleGraph V),
    53‚Üí      G.chromaticNumber = 4 ‚ąß
    54‚Üí        ‚ąÄ m‚āĀ ‚ąą cycleLengths G, ‚ąÄ m‚āā ‚ąą cycleLengths G,
    55‚Üí          m‚āĀ < m‚āā ‚Üí C ‚Č§ m‚āā - m‚āĀ) := by
    56‚Üí  sorry

    58‚Üí/-- **Bondy‚ÄďVince Theorem**: Every graph with minimum degree at least $3$ contains two
    59‚Üícycles whose lengths differ by at most $2$. This is the stronger result that resolves
    60‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 751.
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí[BoVi98] -/
    63‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    64‚Üítheorem erdos_751.variants.bondy_vince {V : Type*} [Fintype V] [DecidableEq V]

    66‚Üí    (hmin : ‚ąÄ v : V, 3 ‚Č§ G.degree v) :
    67‚Üí    ‚ąÉ m‚āĀ ‚ąą cycleLengths G, ‚ąÉ m‚āā ‚ąą cycleLengths G,
    68‚Üí      m‚āĀ < m‚āā ‚ąß m‚āā - m‚āĀ ‚Č§ 2 := by
    69‚Üí  sorry

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 740
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/740](https://www.erdosproblems.com/740)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA question of ErdŇĎs and Hajnal [Er69b][Er71, p.100][Er81][Er95d].
    25‚Üí
    26‚ÜíLet $\mathfrak{m}$ be an infinite cardinal and $G$ be a graph with chromatic number
    27‚Üí$\mathfrak{m}$. Let $r \geq 1$. Must $G$ contain a subgraph of chromatic number
    28‚Üí$\mathfrak{m}$ which does not contain any odd cycle of length $\leq r$?
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíR√∂dl proved this is true if $\mathfrak{m} = \aleph_0$ and $r = 3$ (see [108] for
    31‚Üíthe finitary version).
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíMore generally, ErdŇĎs and Hajnal asked whether for every cardinal $\mathfrak{m}$ and
    34‚Üíinteger $r$, there exists $f_r(\mathfrak{m})$ such that every graph with chromatic
    35‚Üínumber $\geq f_r(\mathfrak{m})$ contains a subgraph with chromatic number
    36‚Üí$\mathfrak{m}$ with no odd cycle of length $\leq r$.
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Cardinal
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üíuniverse u
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üínamespace Erdos740
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The cardinal chromatic number of a graph: the infimum of cardinals $\kappa$
    46‚Üífor which $G$ admits a proper $\kappa$-coloring. -/
    47‚Üínoncomputable def cardChromaticNumber {V : Type u}
    48‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : Cardinal.{u} :=
    49‚Üí  sInf {őļ : Cardinal.{u} | ‚ąÉ (őĪ : Type u), #őĪ = őļ ‚ąß Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ)}
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/--
    52‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 740 [Er69b][Er71][Er81][Er95d]:
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíIf $G$ is a graph with infinite chromatic number $\mathfrak{m}$, then for every
    55‚Üí$r \geq 1$, $G$ contains a subgraph with chromatic number $\mathfrak{m}$ that has
    56‚Üíno odd cycle of length $\leq r$.
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíA question of ErdŇĎs and Hajnal. R√∂dl proved the case
    59‚Üí$\mathfrak{m} = \aleph_0$, $r = 3$.
    60‚Üí-/
    61‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    62‚Üítheorem erdos_740 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    63‚Üí    ‚ąÄ {V : Type u} (G : SimpleGraph V) (ūĚĒ™ : Cardinal.{u}),
    64‚Üí      ‚ĄĶ‚āÄ ‚Č§ ūĚĒ™ ‚Üí cardChromaticNumber G = ūĚĒ™ ‚Üí ‚ąÄ (r : ‚Ąē), 1 ‚Č§ r ‚Üí
    65‚Üí        ‚ąÉ (W : Type u) (H : SimpleGraph W),
    66‚Üí          cardChromaticNumber H = ūĚĒ™ ‚ąß
    67‚Üí          (‚ąÉ f : W ‚Üí V, Function.Injective f ‚ąß ‚ąÄ a b, H.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj (f a) (f b)) ‚ąß
    68‚Üí          (‚ąÄ (w : W) (p : H.Walk w w), p.IsCycle ‚Üí Odd p.length ‚Üí r < p.length) := by
    69‚Üí  sorry
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üíend Erdos740

    26‚ÜíThe original conjecture of ErdŇĎs, Pach, Pollack, and Tuza [EPPT89] stated:
    27‚Üí- If $G$ contains no $K_{2r}$ and $(r-1)(3r+2) \mid d$ then
    28‚Üí  $D \leq \frac{2(r-1)(3r+2)}{2r^2-1} \cdot \frac{n}{d} + O(1)$
    29‚Üí- If $G$ contains no $K_{2r+1}$ and $(3r-1) \mid d$ then
    30‚Üí  $D \leq \frac{3r-1}{r} \cdot \frac{n}{d} + O(1)$
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíThe first case was disproven for $r \geq 2$ by Czabarka, Singgih, and Sz√©kely [CSS21].
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThe amended conjecture (due to [CSS21]) states that if $G$ contains no $K_{k+1}$ then
    35‚Üí$D \leq (3 - 2/k) \cdot n/d + O(1)$.
    36‚Üí
    37‚ÜíThis is known:
    38‚Üí- For $k = 2$ ($K_3$-free), proved in [EPPT89]
    39‚Üí- For $3$-colourable graphs (weaker than $K_4$-free), by Czabarka, Dankelmann,
    40‚Üí  Sz√©kely [CDS09]
    41‚Üí- For $4$-colourable graphs (weaker than $K_5$-free), by Czabarka, Smith,
    42‚Üí  Sz√©kely [CSS23]
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíIt is also known that any connected graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d$ has
    45‚Üí$D \leq 3n/(d+1) + O(1)$.
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí[EPPT89] ErdŇĎs, P., Pach, J., Pollack, R., and Tuza, Zs., _Radius, diameter, and
    48‚Üíminimum degree_. J. Combin. Theory Ser. B 47 (1989), 73-79.
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí[CSS21] Czabarka, √Č., Singgih, O., and Sz√©kely, L.A., _Counterexample to a conjecture
    51‚Üíof ErdŇĎs, Pach, Pollack, and Tuza on diameter and minimum degree_. 2021.
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí[CDS09] Czabarka, √Č., Dankelmann, P., and Sz√©kely, L.A., _Diameter and degree
    54‚Üíconditions for $K_{t,t}$-free graphs_. 2009.
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí[CSS23] Czabarka, √Č., Smith, S.J., and Sz√©kely, L.A., _Diameter bounds for
    57‚Üí4-colourable graphs_. 2023.
    58‚Üí-/

    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 612** (Amended conjecture, [CSS21]):
    66‚Üí
    67‚ÜíIf $G$ is a connected $K_{k+1}$-free graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d \geq 1$,
    68‚Üíthen
    69‚Üí$$\operatorname{diam}(G) \leq \left(3 - \frac{2}{k}\right) \cdot \frac{n}{d} + C$$
    70‚Üífor some constant $C$ depending only on $k$.
    71‚Üí-/

    73‚Üítheorem erdos_612 (k : ‚Ąē) (hk : k ‚Č• 2) :
    74‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    75‚Üí      G.Connected ‚Üí
    76‚Üí      G.CliqueFree (k + 1) ‚Üí
    77‚Üí      G.minDegree ‚Č• 1 ‚Üí
    78‚Üí      (G.diam : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (3 - 2 / (k : ‚ĄĚ)) * ((n : ‚ĄĚ) / (G.minDegree : ‚ĄĚ)) + C := by
    79‚Üí  sorry

    82‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 612** (Known case $k = 2$, [EPPT89]):
    83‚Üí
    84‚ÜíIf $G$ is a connected triangle-free graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree $d \geq 1$,

    24‚ÜíErdŇĎs asked whether there exists a constant $c > 0$ such that for arbitrarily large $n$,
    25‚Üíthere is a $4$-chromatic critical graph on $n$ vertices with minimum degree at least $c \cdot n$.

    42‚Üíthan $k$.

    51‚ÜíIs there a constant $c > 0$ such that for arbitrarily large $n$, there exists

    28‚ÜíErdŇĎs‚ÄďR√©nyi and independently Brown showed that for $n = q^2 + q + 1$ with $q$ a
    29‚Üíprime power, $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) \geq \frac{q(q+1)^2}{2}$, which together with Reiman's
    30‚Üíupper bound gives $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) \sim \frac{1}{2} n^{3/2}$.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[Er93] ErdŇĎs, P., *On some of my favourite theorems*. Combinatorics, Paul ErdŇĎs is eighty,
    33‚ÜíVol. 2 (Keszthely, 1993), 97‚Äď132.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí[MaYa23] Ma, J. and Yang, T., *On the extremal number of subdivisions*. Combinatorica 43
    36‚Üí(2023), 1019‚Äď1027.
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üínamespace Erdos765
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- The cycle graph $C_m$ on $m$ vertices ($m \geq 3$). -/
    44‚Üídef cycleGraph (m : ‚Ąē) (_ : m ‚Č• 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
    45‚Üí  Adj i j := i ‚Č† j ‚ąß (j.val = (i.val + 1) % m ‚ą® i.val = (j.val + 1) % m)
    46‚Üí  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inl‚ü©
    47‚Üí  loopless := fun _ ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- A graph $G$ contains $H$ as a subgraph via an injective graph homomorphism. -/
    50‚Üídef ContainsSubgraph {V U : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph U) : Prop :=
    51‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : U ‚Üí V, Function.Injective f ‚ąß ‚ąÄ u v : U, H.Adj u v ‚Üí G.Adj (f u) (f v)
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- The Tur√°n extremal number $\mathrm{ex}(n; H)$: the maximum number of edges in a
    54‚Üísimple graph on $n$ vertices that does not contain $H$ as a subgraph. -/
    55‚Üínoncomputable def extremalNumber {U : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph U) (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    56‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    57‚Üí    ¬¨ContainsSubgraph G H ‚ąß G.edgeSet.ncard = m}
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí/--
    60‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 765 (SOLVED):
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíThe asymptotic formula for $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4)$ is
    63‚Üí$\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) \sim \frac{1}{2} n^{3/2}$.
    64‚Üí
    65‚ÜíFormally: for every $\varepsilon > 0$, there exists $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    66‚Üí$$\left|\frac{\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4)}{n^{3/2}} - \frac{1}{2}\right| < \varepsilon.$$
    67‚Üí-/
    68‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    69‚Üítheorem erdos_765 :
    70‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    71‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
    72‚Üí      |(‚ÜĎ(extremalNumber (cycleGraph 4 (by omega)) n) / (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((3 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) - 1 / 2)| < őĶ := by
    73‚Üí  sorry
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí/--
    76‚ÜíErdŇĎs's refined conjecture from [Er93] (still open):
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí$\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) = \frac{n^{3/2}}{2} + O(n)$.
    79‚Üí
    80‚ÜíFormally: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all $n \geq 1$,
    81‚Üí$$\left|\mathrm{ex}(n; C_4) - \frac{n^{3/2}}{2}\right| \leq C \cdot n.$$
    82‚Üí
    83‚ÜíHis even stronger conjecture with $n/4$ second-order term was disproved by Ma‚ÄďYang [MaYa23].
    84‚Üí-/
    85‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    86‚Üítheorem erdos_765.variants.refined :
    87‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < C ‚ąß
    88‚Üí    ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, 1 ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
    89‚Üí      |(‚ÜĎ(extremalNumber (cycleGraph 4 (by omega)) n) - (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((3 : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) / 2)| ‚Č§
    90‚Üí        C * (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    91‚Üí  sorry
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üíend Erdos765
    94‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 759
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/759](https://www.erdosproblems.com/759)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíThe cochromatic number of $G$, denoted by $\zeta(G)$, is the minimum number of colours
    25‚Üíneeded to colour the vertices of $G$ such that each colour class induces either
    26‚Üía complete graph or an empty graph.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíLet $z(S_n)$ be the maximum value of $\zeta(G)$ over all graphs $G$ which can be embedded
    29‚Üíon $S_n$, the orientable surface of genus $n$. Determine the growth rate of $z(S_n)$.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs and Gimbel [ErGi93]. Gimbel [Gi86] proved that
    32‚Üí$$\sqrt{n} / \log n \ll z(S_n) \ll \sqrt{n}.$$
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíSolved by Gimbel and Thomassen [GiTh97], who proved
    35‚Üí$$z(S_n) \asymp \sqrt{n} / \log n.$$
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí[ErGi93] ErdŇĎs, P. and Gimbel, J., *Some problems and results in cochromatic theory*, 1993.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí[Gi86] Gimbel, J., *Some remarks on the cochromatic number of a graph*, 1986.
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí[GiTh97] Gimbel, J. and Thomassen, C., *Coloring graphs with fixed genus and girth*, 1997.
    42‚Üí-/
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Real
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üínamespace Erdos759
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- A colouring $c : V \to \operatorname{Fin}(k)$ is a cochromatic colouring of $G$ if every colour
    49‚Üíclass induces either a complete subgraph or an independent set. -/
    50‚Üídef IsCochromaticColouring {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (k : ‚Ąē)
    51‚Üí    (c : V ‚Üí Fin k) : Prop :=
    52‚Üí  ‚ąÄ i : Fin k,
    53‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí G.Adj u v) ‚ą®
    54‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v, c u = i ‚Üí c v = i ‚Üí u ‚Č† v ‚Üí ¬¨G.Adj u v)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- The cochromatic number $\zeta(G)$ of a finite graph $G$: the minimum number of
    57‚Üícolours in a cochromatic colouring. -/
    58‚Üínoncomputable def cochromaticNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    59‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē :=
    60‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ c : V ‚Üí Fin k, IsCochromaticColouring G k c}
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/-- Embeddability of a finite simple graph on the orientable surface of genus $n$.
    63‚ÜíThis is axiomatized since surface topology is not available in Mathlib. -/
    64‚Üíopaque IsEmbeddableOnSurface {m : ‚Ąē} (_ : SimpleGraph (Fin m)) (_ : ‚Ąē) : Prop
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí/-- $z(S_n)$: the maximum cochromatic number over all finite graphs embeddable
    67‚Üíon the orientable surface of genus $n$. -/
    68‚Üínoncomputable def maxCochromaticOnSurface (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    69‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ (m : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin m)),
    70‚Üí    IsEmbeddableOnSurface G n ‚ąß cochromaticNumber G = k}
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üí/--
    73‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 759, upper bound (Gimbel‚ÄďThomassen [GiTh97]):
    74‚Üí
    75‚ÜíThere exist $C > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    76‚Üí$$z(S_n) \leq C \cdot \sqrt{n} / \log n.$$
    77‚Üí-/
    78‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    79‚Üítheorem erdos_759 :
    80‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    81‚Üí      (maxCochromaticOnSurface n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§
    82‚Üí        C * Real.sqrt (n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    83‚Üí  sorry
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/--
    86‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 759, lower bound (Gimbel [Gi86]):
    87‚Üí
    88‚ÜíThere exist $C > 0$ and $N_0$ such that for all $n \geq N_0$,
    89‚Üí$$z(S_n) \geq C \cdot \sqrt{n} / \log n.$$
    90‚Üí-/
    91‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    92‚Üítheorem erdos_759.variants.lower_bound :
    93‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
    94‚Üí      (maxCochromaticOnSurface n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č•
    95‚Üí        C * Real.sqrt (n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    96‚Üí  sorry
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üíend Erdos759

50‚Üídef cycleSpectrum {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    51‚Üí  {‚Ąď : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ v : V, ‚ąÉ p : G.Walk v v, p.IsCycle ‚ąß p.length = ‚Ąď}
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- The number of distinct cycle spectra realizable by simple graphs on $\operatorname{Fin}(n)$. -/
    54‚Üínoncomputable def cycleSetCount (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    55‚Üí  Set.ncard {A : Set ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), cycleSpectrum G = A}
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí/--
    58‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 84, Part 2 (open) [Er94b][Er95][Er96][Er97d]:
    59‚Üí$f(n) / 2^{n/2} \to \infty$.
    60‚ÜíThat is, for every $B > 0$, for all sufficiently large $n$, $f(n) \geq B \cdot 2^{n/2}$.
    61‚Üí-/
    62‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    63‚Üítheorem erdos_84 :
    64‚Üí    ‚ąÄ B : ‚ĄĚ, B > 0 ‚Üí
    65‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
    66‚Üí      (cycleSetCount n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• B * (2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((n : ‚ĄĚ) / 2) := by
    67‚Üí  sorry
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí/--
    70‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 84, Part 1 (proved by Verstra√ęte [Ve04]):
    71‚Üí$f(n) = o(2^n)$.
    72‚ÜíThat is, for every $\varepsilon > 0$, for all sufficiently large $n$,
    73‚Üí$f(n) \leq \varepsilon \cdot 2^n$.
    74‚Üí
    75‚ÜíVerstra√ęte proved $f(n) \ll 2^{n - n^{1/10}}$, improved by Nenadov to
    76‚Üí$f(n) \ll 2^{n - n^{1/2 - o(1)}}$.
    77‚Üí-/
    78‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    79‚Üítheorem erdos_84.variants.subexponential :
    80‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    81‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, N ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
    82‚Üí      (cycleSetCount n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ őĶ * (2 : ‚ĄĚ) ^ n := by
    83‚Üí  sorry
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üíend Erdos84

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 923
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíIs it true that, for every $k$, there is some $f(k)$ such that if $G$ has chromatic number
    23‚Üí$\geq f(k)$ then $G$ contains a triangle-free subgraph with chromatic number $\geq k$?
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/923](https://www.erdosproblems.com/923)
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Ro77] R√∂dl, V., *On the chromatic number of subgraphs of a given graph*, Proc. Amer.
    28‚ÜíMath. Soc. 64 (1977), 370-371.
    29‚Üí-/
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üínamespace Erdos923
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/--
    36‚ÜíIs it true that, for every $k$, there is some $f(k)$ such that if $G$ has chromatic number
    37‚Üí$\geq f(k)$ then $G$ contains a triangle-free subgraph with chromatic number $\geq k$?
    38‚Üí
    39‚ÜíProved by R√∂dl [Ro77]. This is the $r = 4$ special case of problem \#108.
    40‚ÜíTriangle-free is formalized here as girth $\geq 4$ (no cycles of length 3).
    41‚Üí-/
    42‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    43‚Üítheorem erdos_923 :
    44‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    45‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (k : ‚Ąē), 2 ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
    46‚Üí        ‚ąÉ (f : ‚Ąē),
    47‚Üí          ‚ąÄ (V : Type*) (G : SimpleGraph V),
    48‚Üí            (f : ‚Ąē‚ąě) ‚Č§ G.chromaticNumber ‚Üí
    49‚Üí            ‚ąÉ H : G.Subgraph,
    50‚Üí              (k : ‚Ąē‚ąě) ‚Č§ H.coe.chromaticNumber ‚ąß
    51‚Üí              (4 : ‚Ąē‚ąě) ‚Č§ H.coe.egirth := by
    52‚Üí  sorry
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üíend Erdos923

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 842
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/842](https://www.erdosproblems.com/842)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph on $3n$ vertices formed by taking $n$ vertex disjoint triangles
    25‚Üíand adding a Hamiltonian cycle (with all new edges) between these vertices.
    26‚ÜíDoes $G$ have chromatic number at most $3$?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíThe answer is yes, proved by Fleischner and Stiebitz [FlSt92].
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[Er92b] ErdŇĎs, P., _Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics_.
    31‚ÜíMatematiche (Catania) 47 (1992), 231-240.
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[FlSt92] Fleischner, H. and Stiebitz, M., _A solution to a colouring problem of P. ErdŇĎs_.
    34‚ÜíDiscrete Math. 101 (1992), 39-48.
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üínamespace Erdos842
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- The graph of $n$ vertex-disjoint triangles on $3n$ vertices. Vertices $3i$, $3i+1$, $3i+2$
    42‚Üí    form the $i$-th triangle. -/
    43‚Üídef triangleGraph (n : ‚Ąē) : SimpleGraph (Fin (3 * n)) where
    44‚Üí  Adj u v := u ‚Č† v ‚ąß u.val / 3 = v.val / 3
    45‚Üí  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, h.symm‚ü©
    46‚Üí  loopless := fun _ ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- A permutation $\sigma$ on `Fin m` is a single $m$-cycle (Hamiltonian cycle): applying $\sigma$
    49‚Üí    repeatedly from any vertex can reach every other vertex. -/
    50‚Üídef IsSingleCycle {m : ‚Ąē} (ŌÉ : Equiv.Perm (Fin m)) : Prop :=
    51‚Üí  ‚ąÄ u v : Fin m, ‚ąÉ k : ‚Ąē, (ŌÉ ^ k) u = v
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- The cycle graph induced by a permutation $\sigma$: vertices $u$ and $v$ are adjacent
    54‚Üí    iff $\sigma(u) = v$ or $\sigma(v) = u$. -/
    55‚Üídef cycleGraphOfPerm {m : ‚Ąē} (ŌÉ : Equiv.Perm (Fin m)) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
    56‚Üí  Adj u v := u ‚Č† v ‚ąß (ŌÉ u = v ‚ą® ŌÉ v = u)
    57‚Üí  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inl‚ü©
    58‚Üí  loopless := fun _ ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/--
    61‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 842 [Er92b]:
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíLet $G$ be a graph on $3n$ vertices formed by taking $n$ vertex-disjoint triangles
    64‚Üíand adding a Hamiltonian cycle (with all new edges). Then $G$ has chromatic
    65‚Üínumber at most $3$.
    66‚Üí
    67‚ÜíProved by Fleischner and Stiebitz [FlSt92].
    68‚Üí-/
    69‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    70‚Üítheorem erdos_842 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    71‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē), n ‚Č• 1 ‚Üí
    72‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (ŌÉ : Equiv.Perm (Fin (3 * n))),
    73‚Üí    IsSingleCycle ŌÉ ‚Üí
    74‚Üí    (‚ąÄ u v : Fin (3 * n),
    75‚Üí      (cycleGraphOfPerm ŌÉ).Adj u v ‚Üí ¬¨(triangleGraph n).Adj u v) ‚Üí
    76‚Üí    (triangleGraph n ‚äĒ cycleGraphOfPerm ŌÉ).chromaticNumber ‚Č§ 3 := by
    77‚Üí  sorry
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üíend Erdos842
    80‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 811
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/811](https://www.erdosproblems.com/811)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíSuppose $n \equiv 1 \pmod{m}$. We say that an edge-colouring of $K_n$ using $m$ colours
    25‚Üíis balanced if every vertex sees exactly $\lfloor n/m \rfloor$ edges of each colour.
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíFor which graphs $G$ is it true that, if $m = e(G)$, for all large $n \equiv 1 \pmod{m}$,
    28‚Üíevery balanced edge-colouring of $K_n$ with $m$ colours contains a rainbow copy
    29‚Üíof $G$? (That is, a subgraph isomorphic to $G$ where each edge receives a
    30‚Üídifferent colour.)
    31‚Üí
    32‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs, Pyber, and Tuza.
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[Er91] ErdŇĎs, P., _Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics_, 1991.
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[ErTu93] ErdŇĎs, P. and Tuza, Zs., _Rainbow subgraphs in edge-colorings of complete graphs_, 1993.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí[Er96] ErdŇĎs, P., _Some recent problems and results in graph theory_, 1996.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[ClWa23] Clemen, F. and Wagner, A., 2023.
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí[AxCl24] Axenovich, M. and Clemen, F., 2024.
    43‚Üí-/
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Finset
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üínamespace Erdos811
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- The cycle graph on $m$ vertices (for $m \ge 3$): vertex $i$ is adjacent
    50‚Üí    to vertices $(i+1) \bmod m$ and $(i-1) \bmod m$. -/
    51‚Üídef cycleGraph811 (m : ‚Ąē) (_ : m ‚Č• 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
    52‚Üí  Adj i j := i ‚Č† j ‚ąß (j.val = (i.val + 1) % m ‚ą® i.val = (j.val + 1) % m)
    53‚Üí  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inl‚ü©
    54‚Üí  loopless := fun _ ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- An edge-colouring of the complete graph on $\operatorname{Fin} n$ with $m$ colours is
    57‚Üí    **balanced** if every vertex sees exactly $\lfloor n / m \rfloor$ edges of each colour.
    58‚Üí    (When $n \equiv 1 \pmod{m}$, the degree $n - 1$ divides evenly as
    59‚Üí    $m \cdot \lfloor n / m \rfloor$.) -/
    60‚Üídef IsBalancedColoring (n m : ‚Ąē) (c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin m) : Prop :=
    61‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (v : Fin n) (i : Fin m),
    62‚Üí    (Finset.univ.filter (fun w : Fin n => w ‚Č† v ‚ąß
    63‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (v, w)) = i)).card = n / m
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí/-- A **rainbow copy** of a graph $G$ in a coloured complete graph on $\operatorname{Fin} n$
    66‚Üí    is an injective vertex map such that all edges of $G$ receive pairwise
    67‚Üí    distinct colours. -/
    68‚Üídef HasRainbowCopy {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V)
    69‚Üí    {n m : ‚Ąē} (c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin m) : Prop :=
    70‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : V ‚Üí Fin n, Function.Injective f ‚ąß
    71‚Üí    ‚ąÄ a b a' b' : V, G.Adj a b ‚Üí G.Adj a' b' ‚Üí
    72‚Üí      Sym2.mk (a, b) ‚Č† Sym2.mk (a', b') ‚Üí
    73‚Üí      c (Sym2.mk (f a, f b)) ‚Č† c (Sym2.mk (f a', f b'))
    74‚Üí
    75‚Üí/--
    76‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 811** (specific challenge from [Er91] and [Er96]):
    77‚Üí
    78‚ÜíFor all sufficiently large $n \equiv 1 \pmod{6}$, every balanced edge-colouring
    79‚Üíof $K_n$ with $6$ colours contains a rainbow copy of $C_6$.
    80‚Üí
    81‚ÜíThe companion question about $K_4$ (which also has $6$ edges) was answered
    82‚Üínegatively by Clemen and Wagner [ClWa23].
    83‚Üí-/
    84‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    85‚Üítheorem erdos_811 :
    86‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N ‚Üí n % 6 = 1 ‚Üí
    87‚Üí      ‚ąÄ c : Sym2 (Fin n) ‚Üí Fin 6,
    88‚Üí        IsBalancedColoring n 6 c ‚Üí
    89‚Üí        HasRainbowCopy (cycleGraph811 6 (by omega)) c := by
    90‚Üí  sorry
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üíend Erdos811
    93‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 108
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/108](https://www.erdosproblems.com/108)

    33‚Üícontains a subgraph of girth ‚Č• r and chromatic number ‚Č• k?

    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 626
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/626](https://www.erdosproblems.com/626)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $k \geq 4$ and $g_k(n)$ denote the largest $m$ such that there is a graph on $n$ vertices
    25‚Üíwith chromatic number $k$ and girth $> m$. Does
    26‚Üí$$
    27‚Üí  \lim_{n \to \infty} g_k(n) / \log n
    28‚Üí$$
    29‚Üíexist?
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíConversely, if $h^{(m)}(n)$ is the maximal chromatic number of a graph on $n$ vertices
    32‚Üíwith girth $> m$ then does
    33‚Üí$$
    34‚Üí  \lim_{n \to \infty} \log h^{(m)}(n) / \log n
    35‚Üí$$
    36‚Üíexist, and what is its value?
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí[Er59b] ErdŇĎs, P., _Graph theory and probability_, Canad. J. Math., 1959.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[Er62b] ErdŇĎs, P., _On circuits and subgraphs of chromatic graphs_, Mathematika, 1962.
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí[Er69b] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems and results in chromatic graph theory_, 1969.
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí[Ko88] Kostochka, A.V., _The minimum Hadwiger number for graphs with a given mean degree of
    45‚Üívertices_, Metody Diskret. Analiz., 1988.
    46‚Üí-/
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Filter
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üíopen scoped Topology
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üínamespace Erdos626
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üíattribute [local instance] Classical.propDecidable
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- The chromatic number of a simple graph on $\text{Fin}(n)$: the minimum number of colors $k$
    57‚Üí    such that there exists a proper coloring $f : \text{Fin}(n) \to \text{Fin}(k)$. -/
    58‚Üínoncomputable def finChromaticNumber {n : ‚Ąē} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : ‚Ąē :=
    59‚Üí  sInf {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ f : Fin n ‚Üí Fin k, ‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí f u ‚Č† f v}
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí/-- A graph on $\text{Fin}(n)$ has girth greater than $m$ if every cycle has length
    62‚Üí    strictly greater than $m$. -/
    63‚Üídef HasGirthGt {n : ‚Ąē} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) (m : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    64‚Üí  ‚ąÄ v : Fin n, ‚ąÄ p : G.Walk v v, p.IsCycle ‚Üí m < p.length
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí/-- $g_k(n)$ is the largest $m$ such that there exists a graph on $n$ vertices
    67‚Üí    with chromatic number $k$ and girth $> m$. Returns $0$ if no such graph exists. -/
    68‚Üínoncomputable def g (k n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    69‚Üí  sSup {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), finChromaticNumber G = k ‚ąß HasGirthGt G m}
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- $h^{(m)}(n)$ is the maximal chromatic number of a graph on $n$ vertices
    72‚Üí    with girth $> m$. -/
    73‚Üínoncomputable def h (m n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    74‚Üí  sSup {k : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), finChromaticNumber G = k ‚ąß HasGirthGt G m}
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/--
    77‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 626, Part 1** [Er59b][Er62b][Er69b]:
    78‚Üí
    79‚ÜíFor $k \geq 4$, does the limit $\lim_{n \to \infty} g_k(n) / \log n$ exist?
    80‚Üí
    81‚ÜíIt is known that
    82‚Üí$$
    83‚Üí  \frac{1}{4 \log k} \log n \leq g_k(n) \leq \frac{2}{\log(k-2)} \log n + 1,
    84‚Üí$$
    85‚Üíthe lower bound due to Kostochka [Ko88] and the upper bound to ErdŇĎs [Er59b].
    86‚Üí-/
    87‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    88‚Üítheorem erdos_626 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    89‚Üí    ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k ‚Č• 4 ‚Üí ‚ąÉ L : ‚ĄĚ,
    90‚Üí      Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ąē => (g k n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)) atTop (nhds L) := by
    91‚Üí  sorry
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/--
    94‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 626, Part 2** [Er59b][Er62b][Er69b]:
    95‚Üí
    96‚ÜíFor $m \geq 1$, does the limit $\lim_{n \to \infty} \log h^{(m)}(n) / \log n$ exist,
    97‚Üíand what is its value?
    98‚Üí
    99‚ÜíErdŇĎs [Er59b] proved that
   100‚Üí$$
   101‚Üí  \lim_{n \to \infty} \frac{\log h^{(m)}(n)}{\log n} \gg \frac{1}{m}
   102‚Üí$$
   103‚Üíand, for odd $m$,
   104‚Üí$$
   105‚Üí  \lim_{n \to \infty} \frac{\log h^{(m)}(n)}{\log n} \leq \frac{2}{m+1},
   106‚Üí$$
   107‚Üíand conjectured this is sharp.
   108‚Üí-/
   109‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
   110‚Üítheorem erdos_626.variants.maximal_chromatic : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
   111‚Üí    ‚ąÄ m : ‚Ąē, m ‚Č• 1 ‚Üí ‚ąÉ L : ‚ĄĚ,
   112‚Üí      Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ąē => Real.log (h m n : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ)) atTop (nhds L) := by
   113‚Üí  sorry
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üíend Erdos626
   116‚Üí

    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 62
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/62](https://www.erdosproblems.com/62)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIf $G_1$ and $G_2$ are graphs with chromatic number $\aleph_1$, must they share a common
    25‚Üísubgraph with infinite chromatic number?
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[EHS74] ErdŇĎs, P., Hajnal, A., and Shelah, S., *On some general properties of chromatic numbers*.
    28‚ÜíTopics in topology (Proc. Colloq., Keszthely, 1972), Colloq. Math. Soc. Janos Bolyai (1974),
    29‚Üí243-255.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Er87] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some problems on finite and infinite graphs*.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Cardinal
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos62
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- A graph $G$ has chromatic number $\aleph_1$ if it cannot be properly colored by any
    39‚Üícountable set of colors, but can be colored by a set of cardinality $\aleph_1$. -/
    40‚Üídef HasChromaticNumberAleph1 {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
    41‚Üí  (‚ąÄ (őĪ : Type*) [Countable őĪ], IsEmpty (G.Coloring őĪ)) ‚ąß
    42‚Üí  (‚ąÉ őĪ : Type*, #őĪ = aleph 1 ‚ąß Nonempty (G.Coloring őĪ))
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- $G$ contains $H$ as a subgraph via an injective adjacency-preserving map. -/
    45‚Üídef ContainsSubgraph {V U : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph U) : Prop :=
    46‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : U ‚Üí V, Function.Injective f ‚ąß ‚ąÄ u v : U, H.Adj u v ‚Üí G.Adj (f u) (f v)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/--
    49‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 62 (strong version) [Er87]:
    50‚Üí
    51‚ÜíIf $G_1$, $G_2$ are two graphs with chromatic number $\aleph_1$, must there exist a graph $H$
    52‚Üíwith infinite chromatic number ($\chi \geq \aleph_0$) which is a subgraph of both $G_1$ and $G_2$?
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíThis is the stronger form of the conjecture. ErdŇĎs also asked [Er87] about
    55‚Üífinding such a common subgraph in any finite collection of graphs with
    56‚Üíchromatic number $\aleph_1$.
    57‚Üí-/
    58‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    59‚Üítheorem erdos_62 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    60‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (V‚āĀ : Type*) (V‚āā : Type*) (G‚āĀ : SimpleGraph V‚āĀ) (G‚āā : SimpleGraph V‚āā),
    61‚Üí    HasChromaticNumberAleph1 G‚āĀ ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    HasChromaticNumberAleph1 G‚āā ‚Üí
    63‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (U : Type*) (H : SimpleGraph U),
    64‚Üí      H.chromaticNumber = ‚ä§ ‚ąß
    65‚Üí      ContainsSubgraph G‚āĀ H ‚ąß
    66‚Üí      ContainsSubgraph G‚āā H := by
    67‚Üí  sorry
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí/--
    70‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 62 (weak version) [Er87]:
    71‚Üí
    72‚ÜíIf $G_1$, $G_2$ are two graphs with chromatic number $\aleph_1$, must there exist a graph $H$
    73‚Üíwith chromatic number at least $4$ which is a subgraph of both $G_1$ and $G_2$?
    74‚Üí
    75‚ÜíEvery graph with chromatic number $\aleph_1$ contains all sufficiently large odd
    76‚Üícycles (chromatic number $3$), proved by ErdŇĎs, Hajnal, and Shelah [EHS74].
    77‚ÜíErdŇĎs wrote [Er87] that 'probably' every graph with chromatic number $\aleph_1$
    78‚Üícontains as subgraphs all graphs with chromatic number $4$ with sufficiently
    79‚Üílarge girth.
    80‚Üí-/
    81‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    82‚Üítheorem erdos_62.variants.weak : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    83‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (V‚āĀ : Type*) (V‚āā : Type*) (G‚āĀ : SimpleGraph V‚āĀ) (G‚āā : SimpleGraph V‚āā),
    84‚Üí    HasChromaticNumberAleph1 G‚āĀ ‚Üí
    85‚Üí    HasChromaticNumberAleph1 G‚āā ‚Üí
    86‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (U : Type*) (H : SimpleGraph U),
    87‚Üí      ¬¨ H.Colorable 3 ‚ąß
    88‚Üí      ContainsSubgraph G‚āĀ H ‚ąß
    89‚Üí      ContainsSubgraph G‚āā H := by
    90‚Üí  sorry
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üíend Erdos62
    93‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 207
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/207](https://www.erdosproblems.com/207)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíFor any $g \geq 2$, if $n$ is sufficiently large and $n \equiv 1, 3 \pmod{6}$, then there
    25‚Üíexists a Steiner triple system on $n$ vertices with girth greater than $g$ ‚ÄĒ that is,
    26‚Üía $3$-uniform hypergraph where every pair of vertices is in exactly one edge, and
    27‚Üíany $j$ edges (for $2 \leq j \leq g$) span at least $j + 3$ vertices.
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíThe congruence condition $n \equiv 1, 3 \pmod{6}$ is the necessary and sufficient
    30‚Üídivisibility condition for a Steiner triple system on $n$ points to exist.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[KSSS22b] Kwan, M., Sah, A., Sawhney, M., and Simkin, M., _High-girth Steiner
    33‚Üítriple systems_ (2022).
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos207
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- A set of hyperedges forms a Steiner triple system: each edge has exactly $3$
    39‚Üí    vertices, and every pair of distinct vertices appears in exactly one edge. -/
    40‚Üídef IsSteinerTripleSystem {n : ‚Ąē} (edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n))) : Prop :=
    41‚Üí  (‚ąÄ e ‚ąą edges, e.card = 3) ‚ąß
    42‚Üí  (‚ąÄ u v : Fin n, u ‚Č† v ‚Üí ‚ąÉ! e, e ‚ąą edges ‚ąß u ‚ąą e ‚ąß v ‚ąą e)
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/--
    45‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 207 [KSSS22b]:
    46‚Üí
    47‚ÜíFor any $g \geq 2$, if $n$ is sufficiently large and $n \equiv 1$ or $3 \pmod{6}$, there exists
    48‚Üía Steiner triple system on $n$ vertices such that any $j$ edges (for $2 \leq j \leq g$)
    49‚Üíspan at least $j + 3$ vertices.
    50‚Üí
    51‚ÜíThe girth condition forbids short "cycles" in the hypergraph: any collection of
    52‚Üí$j$ edges (for $2 \leq j \leq g$) must contain at least $j + 3$ distinct vertices.
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíProved by Kwan, Sah, Sawhney, and Simkin [KSSS22b].
    55‚Üí-/
    56‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    57‚Üítheorem erdos_207 :
    58‚Üí    ‚ąÄ g : ‚Ąē, 2 ‚Č§ g ‚Üí
    59‚Üí      ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
    60‚Üí        (n % 6 = 1 ‚ą® n % 6 = 3) ‚Üí
    61‚Üí          ‚ąÉ edges : Finset (Finset (Fin n)),
    62‚Üí            IsSteinerTripleSystem edges ‚ąß
    63‚Üí            ‚ąÄ j : ‚Ąē, 2 ‚Č§ j ‚Üí j ‚Č§ g ‚Üí
    64‚Üí              ‚ąÄ S : Finset (Finset (Fin n)), S ‚äÜ edges ‚Üí S.card = j ‚Üí
    65‚Üí                j + 3 ‚Č§ (S.biUnion id).card := by
    66‚Üí  sorry
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üíend Erdos207

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 572
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/572](https://www.erdosproblems.com/572)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíShow that for $k \geq 3$,
    25‚Üí$$\mathrm{ex}(n; C_{2k}) \gg n^{1 + 1/k}.$$
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíHere $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_{2k})$ is the Tur√°n extremal number: the maximum number of edges in
    28‚Üían $n$-vertex graph that does not contain the even cycle $C_{2k}$ as a subgraph.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíThe notation $\gg$ means there exists a positive constant $c > 0$ such that
    31‚Üí$\mathrm{ex}(n; C_{2k}) \geq c \cdot n^{1+1/k}$ for all sufficiently large $n$.
    32‚Üí
    33‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs [Er64c]. The upper bound $\mathrm{ex}(n; C_{2k}) \ll k \cdot n^{1+1/k}$
    34‚Üíwas proved by ErdŇĎs [Er64c] and Bondy‚ÄďSimonovits [BoSi74]. The matching lower
    35‚Üíbound is known for $k = 2$ (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďKlein [Er38]), $k = 3, 5$ (Benson [Be66]),
    36‚Üíand $k = 4$ (Wenger). The general case remains open.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí[Er64c] ErdŇĎs, P., _On extremal problems of graphs and generalized graphs_.
    39‚ÜíIsrael J. Math. 2 (1964), 183‚Äď190.
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí[BoSi74] Bondy, J.A. and Simonovits, M., _Cycles of even length in graphs_.
    42‚ÜíJ. Combin. Theory Ser. B 16 (1974), 97‚Äď105.
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí[Er38] ErdŇĎs, P., _On sequences of integers no one of which divides the product
    45‚Üíof two others and on some related problems_. Mitt. Forsch.-Inst. Math. Mech.
    46‚ÜíUniv. Tomsk 2 (1938), 74‚Äď82.
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí[Be66] Benson, C.T., _Minimal regular graphs of girths eight and twelve_.
    49‚ÜíCanad. J. Math. 18 (1966), 1091‚Äď1094.
    50‚Üí-/

    53‚Üí
    54‚Üínamespace Erdos572
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- The cycle graph $C_m$ on $m$ vertices ($m \geq 3$). Vertex $i$ is adjacent to vertex
    57‚Üí$i + 1 \pmod{m}$ and vertex $i - 1 \pmod{m}$. -/
    58‚Üídef cycleGraph (m : ‚Ąē) (_ : m ‚Č• 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where

    27‚Üívariable {őĪ : Type*} [Fintype őĪ] [DecidableEq őĪ]
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí/-- `Ls G` is the maximum number of leaves over all spanning trees of `G`.
    30‚ÜíIt is defined to be `0` when `G` is not connected. -/
    31‚Üínoncomputable def Ls (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    32‚Üí  letI spanningTrees := { T : Subgraph G | T.IsSpanning ‚ąß IsTree T.coe }
    33‚Üí  letI leaves (T : Subgraph G) := T.verts.toFinset.filter (fun v => T.degree v = 1)
    34‚Üí  letI num_leaves (T : Subgraph G) := (leaves T).card
    35‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun T => (num_leaves T : ‚ĄĚ)) spanningTrees)
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- `n G` is the number of vertices of `G` as a real number. -/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def n (_ : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ := Fintype.card őĪ
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- `m G` is the size of a maximum matching of `G`. -/
    41‚Üínoncomputable def m (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    42‚Üí  let matchings := { M : Subgraph G | M.IsMatching }
    43‚Üí  sSup (Set.image (fun M => (M.edgeSet.toFinset.card : ‚ĄĚ)) matchings)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The maximum cardinality among all independent sets `s`
    46‚Üí    that maximize the quantity `|s| - |N(s)|`, where `N(s)`
    47‚Üí    is the neighborhood of the set `s`. -/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def aprime (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    49‚Üí  letI indep_sets : Finset (Finset őĪ) := univ.powerset.filter (fun s => G.IsIndepSet (s : Set őĪ))
    50‚Üí  letI diff (s : Finset őĪ) : ‚Ą§ := (s.card : ‚Ą§) - (‚čÉ v ‚ąą (s : Set őĪ), G.neighborSet v).toFinset.card
    51‚Üí  letI max_diff := (indep_sets.image diff).max
    52‚Üí  letI critical_sets := indep_sets.filter (fun s ‚Ü¶ diff s = max_diff.getD 0)
    53‚Üí  letI max_card := (critical_sets.image Finset.card).max
    54‚Üí  (max_card.getD 0 : ‚ĄĚ)
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- `largestInducedForestSize G` is the size of a largest induced forest of `G`. -/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedForestSize (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    58‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚ąÉ s : Finset őĪ, (G.induce s).IsAcyclic ‚ąß s.card = n }
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- The degree sequence of a graph, sorted in nondecreasing order. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequence (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : List ‚Ąē :=
    62‚Üí  (Finset.univ.val.map fun v : őĪ => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚Č§ ¬∑)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíThe maximum number of occurrences of any term of the degree sequence of `G`.
    66‚Üí-/
    67‚Üínoncomputable def degreeSequenceMultiplicity (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
    68‚Üí  letI degs := degreeSequence G
    69‚Üí  (List.max? (degs.map (fun d => degs.count d))).getD 0
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/-- `largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G` is the size of a largest induced
    72‚Üíbipartite subgraph of `G`. -/
    73‚Üínoncomputable def largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    74‚Üí  sSup { n | ‚ąÉ s : Finset őĪ, (G.induce s).IsBipartite ‚ąß s.card = n }
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/-- `b G` is the number of vertices of a largest induced bipartite subgraph of `G`.
    77‚ÜíReturned as a real number. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def b (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    79‚Üí  (largestInducedBipartiteSubgraphSize G : ‚ĄĚ)
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- Independence number of the neighbourhood of `v`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighborsCard (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    83‚Üí  (G.induce (G.neighborSet v)).indepNum
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
    86‚Üínoncomputable def indepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    87‚Üí  (indepNeighborsCard G v : ‚ĄĚ)
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí/-- Average of `indepNeighbors` over all vertices. -/
    90‚Üínoncomputable def averageIndepNeighbors (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    91‚Üí  (‚ąĎ v ‚ąą Finset.univ, indepNeighbors G v) / (Fintype.card őĪ : ‚ĄĚ)
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí/-- A unit distance graph in ‚ĄĚ¬≤:
    94‚ÜíA graph where the vertices V are a collection of points in ‚ĄĚ¬≤ and there is
    95‚Üían edge between two points if and only if the distance between them is 1. -/
    96‚Üídef UnitDistancePlaneGraph (V : Set (EuclideanSpace ‚ĄĚ (Fin 2))) : SimpleGraph V where
    97‚Üí  Adj x y := Dist.dist x y = 1
    98‚Üí  symm x y := by simp [PseudoMetricSpace.dist_comm]
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíTwo walks are internally disjoint if they share no vertices other than their endpoints.
   102‚Üí-/
   103‚Üídef InternallyDisjoint {V : Type*} {G : SimpleGraph V} {u v x y : V}
   104‚Üí    (p : G.Walk u v) (q : G.Walk x y) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  Disjoint p.support.tail.dropLast q.support.tail.dropLast
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí/--
   108‚ÜíWe say a graph is infinitely connected if any two vertices are connected by infinitely many
   109‚Üípairwise disjoint paths.
   110‚Üí-/
   111‚Üídef InfinitelyConnected {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : Prop :=
   112‚Üí  Pairwise fun u v ‚Ü¶ ‚ąÉ P : Set (G.Walk u v),
   113‚Üí    P.Infinite ‚ąß (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, p.IsPath) ‚ąß P.Pairwise InternallyDisjoint
   114‚Üí
   115‚Üí/-- Infinite graphs: definitions for max degree and clique number so that the maximum
   116‚Üídegree (resp. clique number) of a graph with unbounded degree (resp. clique size) is
   117‚Üí`‚ąě` rather than 0.
   118‚Üí-/
   119‚Üínoncomputable
   120‚Üídef edegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (v : V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := (G.neighborSet v).encard
   121‚Üí
   122‚Üínoncomputable
   123‚Üídef emaxDegree {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := ‚®Ü v, G.edegree v
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üínoncomputable
   126‚Üídef ecliqueNum {V : Type} (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē‚ąě := ‚®Ü (s : Finset V) (_ : G.IsClique s), #s
   127‚Üí
   128‚Üíend SimpleGraph

    33‚Üí/-- Abbreviation for the average independence number of the neighborhoods.
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üínoncomputable abbrev l (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ := averageIndepNeighbors G
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- The same quantity under a different name, used in some conjectures.
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üínoncomputable abbrev l_avg (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ := averageIndepNeighbors G
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- Independent domination number of `G`. -/
    42‚Üínoncomputable def gi (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē := G.indepDominationNumber
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- `temp_v G v = deg(v)/(n(G) - deg(v))`. -/
    45‚Üínoncomputable def temp_v (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (v : őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    46‚Üí  let n := Fintype.card őĪ
    47‚Üí  let deg := G.degree v
    48‚Üí  if n = deg then 0 else (deg : ‚ĄĚ) / ((n : ‚ĄĚ) - (deg : ‚ĄĚ))
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- Maximum of `temp_v` over all vertices. -/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def MaxTemp (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] [Fintype őĪ] [Nonempty őĪ] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    52‚Üí  let temps := Finset.univ.image (temp_v G)
    53‚Üí  temps.max' (Finset.image_nonempty.mpr Finset.univ_nonempty)
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-- Cardinality of the union of the neighbourhoods of the ends of the non-edge `e`. -/
    56‚Üídef non_edge_neighborhood_card (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] (e : Sym2 őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
    57‚Üí  Sym2.lift ‚ü®fun u v => (G.neighborFinset u ‚ą™ G.neighborFinset v).card,
    58‚Üí    fun u v => by simp [Finset.union_comm]‚ü© e
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí/-- Minimum size of the neighbourhood of a non-edge of `G`. -/
    61‚Üínoncomputable def NG (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
    62‚Üí  let non_edges := (compl G).edgeFinset
    63‚Üí  if h : non_edges.Nonempty then
    64‚Üí    let neighbor_sizes := non_edges.image (non_edge_neighborhood_card G)
    65‚Üí    (neighbor_sizes.min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _))
    66‚Üí  else
    67‚Üí    (Fintype.card őĪ : ‚ĄĚ)
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí/-- Size of a largest induced forest of `G` expressed as a real number. -/
    70‚Üínoncomputable def S (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    71‚Üí  let card := Fintype.card őĪ
    72‚Üí  if card < 2 then 0 else
    73‚Üí    let degrees := Multiset.ofList (List.map (fun v => G.degree v) Finset.univ.toList)
    74‚Üí    let sorted_degrees := degrees.sort (¬∑ ‚Č§ ¬∑)
    75‚Üí    ‚ÜĎ((sorted_degrees[card - 2]?).getD 0)
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí/-- Local eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    78‚Üínoncomputable def local_eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ENat :=
    79‚Üí  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí/-- The largest integer less than or equal to `x`. -/
    82‚Üínoncomputable def FLOOR (x : ‚ĄĚ) : ‚ĄĚ := ‚Ćäx‚Ćč
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí/-- Eccentricity of a vertex. -/
    85‚Üínoncomputable def eccentricity (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) : ‚Ąē‚ąě :=
    86‚Üí  sSup (Set.range (G.edist v))
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí/-- The minimum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    89‚Üínoncomputable def minEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē‚ąě :=
    90‚Üí  sInf (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí/-- The set of vertices of minimum eccentricity. -/
    93‚Üínoncomputable def graphCenter (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : Set őĪ :=
    94‚Üí  {v : őĪ | eccentricity G v = minEccentricity G}
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí/-- The maximum eccentricity among all vertices. -/
    97‚Üínoncomputable def maxEccentricity (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē‚ąě :=
    98‚Üí  sSup (Set.range (eccentricity G))
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/-- The set of vertices of maximum eccentricity. -/
   101‚Üínoncomputable def maxEccentricityVertices (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : Set őĪ :=
   102‚Üí  {v : őĪ | eccentricity G v = maxEccentricity G}
   103‚Üí
   104‚Üí/-- Distance from a vertex to a finite set. -/
   105‚Üínoncomputable def distToSet (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (v : őĪ) (S : Set őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
   106‚Üí  if h : S.toFinset.Nonempty then
   107‚Üí    (S.toFinset.image (fun s => G.dist v s)).min' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   108‚Üí  else 0
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí/-- Average distance of `G`. -/
   111‚Üínoncomputable def averageDistance (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
   112‚Üí  if Fintype.card őĪ > 1 then
   113‚Üí    (‚ąĎ u ‚ąą Finset.univ, ‚ąĎ v ‚ąą Finset.univ, (G.dist u v : ‚ĄĚ)) /
   114‚Üí      ((Fintype.card őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) * ((Fintype.card őĪ : ‚ĄĚ) - 1))
   115‚Üí  else
   116‚Üí    0
   117‚Üí
   118‚Üí/-- The floor of the average distance of `G`. -/
   119‚Üínoncomputable def path (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
   120‚Üí  if G.Connected then
   121‚Üí    Nat.floor (averageDistance G)
   122‚Üí  else
   123‚Üí    0
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üí/-- Auxiliary quantity `ecc` used in conjecture 34. -/
   126‚Üínoncomputable def ecc (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (S : Set őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
   127‚Üí  let s_comp := Finset.univ.filter (fun v => v ‚ąČ S)
   128‚Üí  if h : s_comp.Nonempty then
   129‚Üí    (s_comp.image (fun v => distToSet G v S)).max' (Finset.Nonempty.image h _)
   130‚Üí  else 0
   131‚Üí
   132‚Üí/-- Average distance from all vertices to a given set. -/
   133‚Üínoncomputable def distavg (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (S : Set őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ :=
   134‚Üí  if Fintype.card őĪ > 0 then
   135‚Üí    (‚ąĎ v ‚ąą Finset.univ, (distToSet G v S : ‚ĄĚ)) / (Fintype.card őĪ : ‚ĄĚ)
   136‚Üí  else
   137‚Üí    0
   138‚Üí
   139‚Üí/-- A family of paths covering all vertices without overlaps. -/
   140‚Üídef IsPathCover (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (P : Finset (Finset őĪ)) : Prop :=
   141‚Üí  (‚ąÄ s1 ‚ąą P, ‚ąÄ s2 ‚ąą P, s1 ‚Č† s2 ‚Üí Disjoint s1 s2) ‚ąß
   142‚Üí  (Finset.univ ‚äÜ P.biUnion id) ‚ąß
   143‚Üí  (‚ąÄ s ‚ąą P, ‚ąÉ (u v : őĪ) (p : G.Walk u v), p.IsPath ‚ąß s = p.support.toFinset)
   144‚Üí
   145‚Üí/-- Minimum size of a path cover of `G`. -/
   146‚Üínoncomputable def pathCoverNumber (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
   147‚Üí  sInf { k | ‚ąÉ P : Finset (Finset őĪ), P.card = k ‚ąß IsPathCover G P }
   148‚Üí
   149‚Üí/-- The same quantity as a real number. -/
   150‚Üínoncomputable def p (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚ĄĚ := (pathCoverNumber G : ‚ĄĚ)
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí/-- The Wiener index of `G`, which is the sum of distances between all
   153‚Üípairs of vertices. -/
   154‚Üínoncomputable def wienerIndex (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
   155‚Üí  ‚ąĎ uv : Sym2 őĪ, uv.lift ‚ü®fun u v ‚Ü¶ G.dist u v, by simp [dist_comm]‚ü©
   156‚Üí
   157‚Üí/-- Auxiliary function for Szeged index: counts vertices closer to u than v. -/
   158‚Üínoncomputable def szeged_aux (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) (u v : őĪ) : ‚Ąē :=
   159‚Üí  (Finset.univ.filter (fun w => G.edist w u < G.edist w v)).card
   160‚Üí
   161‚Üí/-- The Szeged index of `G`.
   162‚Üí
   163‚ÜíThis is define as the sum `‚ąĎ_{uv ‚ąą E(G)} n_u(u,v) * n_v(u,v)` where
   164‚Üí`n_u(uv)` is the number of vertices closer to `u` than `v`.
   165‚Üí-/
   166‚Üínoncomputable def szegedIndex (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   167‚Üí  ‚ąĎ e ‚ąą G.edgeFinset,
   168‚Üí    e.lift ‚ü®fun u v => szeged_aux G u v * szeged_aux G v u, by simp [mul_comm]‚ü©
   169‚Üí
   170‚Üí/-- The average degree of `G`. -/
   171‚Üínoncomputable def averageDegree (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąö  :=
   172‚Üí  (‚ąĎ v, (G.degree v : ‚Ąö)) / (Fintype.card őĪ : ‚Ąö)
   173‚Üí
   174‚Üí/-- The multiset of degrees of a graph. -/
   175‚Üídef degreeMultiset (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Multiset ‚Ąē :=
   176‚Üí  Finset.univ.val.map fun v => G.degree v
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí/-- The annihilation number of a graph. This is the largest number of degrees that can be added
   179‚Üítogether without going over the total number of edges of that graph. -/
   180‚Üídef annihilationNumber (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   181‚Üí  -- Calculate the limit: The number of edges (Sum of degrees / 2)
   182‚Üí  letI limit := G.edgeFinset.card
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí  -- The set of all multisets of degrees that sum to less than or equal to `limit`
   185‚Üí  Finset.Iic (degreeMultiset G)
   186‚Üí    |>.filter (fun S ‚Ü¶ Multiset.sum S ‚Č§ limit)
   187‚Üí    |>.sup Multiset.card
   188‚Üí
   189‚Üí/--
   190‚ÜíComputes the annihilation number of a graph G.
   191‚Üí
   192‚ÜíIt calculates the degree sequence, sorts it ascendingly, and finds the largest prefix length 'k'
   193‚Üí(where `0 ‚Č§ k ‚Č§ |V(G)|`) such that the sum of the prefix is less than or equal to the sum of the corresponding suffix.
   194‚Üí-/
   195‚Üínoncomputable def annihilationNumber' (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   196‚Üí  -- 1. Get the degree sequence sorted in ascending order.
   197‚Üí  -- G.degree_list returns the list of degrees.
   198‚Üí  letI degrees := (Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚Č§ ¬∑)
   199‚Üí
   200‚Üí  -- 2. Define the condition for the annihilation number.
   201‚Üí  -- k represents the number of smallest degrees considered (the length of the prefix).
   202‚Üí  letI condition (k : ‚Ąē) : Bool := (degrees.take k).sum ‚Č§ (degrees.drop k).sum
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí  -- 3. Find the maximum k in {0, ..., n} satisfying the condition.
   205‚Üí  -- List.range (n + 1) generates the list [0, 1, ..., n].
   206‚Üí  letI candidates := (List.range (Fintype.card őĪ + 1)).filter condition
   207‚Üí
   208‚Üí  -- 4. Return the maximum candidate.
   209‚Üí  -- The list of candidates is guaranteed to be non-empty because k=0 always satisfies
   210‚Üí  -- the condition (0 ‚Č§ sum of all degrees).
   211‚Üí  candidates.getLast!
   212‚Üí
   213‚Üíset_option linter.unusedSectionVars false in
   214‚Üí-- TODO(Paul-Lez): debug the issue with the unused variable linter...
   215‚Üíproof_wanted annihilationNumberEq (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] :
   216‚Üí    annihilationNumber G = annihilationNumber' G

   218‚Üí/-!
   219‚Üí## Residue
   220‚ÜíThe residue of a graph is the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   221‚Üíalgorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   222‚Üí-/
   223‚Üí
   224‚Üí/--
   225‚ÜíHelper function: Performs one step of the Havel-Hakimi reduction on a degree sequence.
   226‚ÜíAssumes the input list `s` is sorted descending.
   227‚ÜíRemoves the first element `d`, decrements the next `d` elements by 1, and re-sorts the list descending.
   228‚Üí
   229‚ÜíNote: when `s` is the list of vertices arising from a simple graph, if the first index is `s` then
   230‚Üíthe degree list always has length at least `s+1` so this makes sense.
   231‚Üí-/
   232‚Üíprivate def havelHakimiStep (s : List ‚Ąē) : List ‚Ąē :=
   233‚Üí  match s with
   234‚Üí  | [] => []
   235‚Üí  | d :: rest =>
   236‚Üí    -- Split the rest into the part to decrement (first d elements) and the remaining part.
   237‚Üí    let (to_decrement, remaining) := rest.splitAt d
   238‚Üí    -- Decrement the elements
   239‚Üí    let decremented := to_decrement.map (¬∑ - 1)
   240‚Üí    -- Combine and re-sort descending.
   241‚Üí    (decremented ++ remaining).mergeSort (¬∑ ‚Č• ¬∑)
   242‚Üí
   243‚Üí/--
   244‚ÜíAuxiliary function to calculate the residue recursively.
   245‚ÜíApplies Havel-Hakimi steps until the sequence consists only of zeros or is empty.
   246‚Üí-/
   247‚Üíprivate partial def residueAux : List ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē
   248‚Üí  | [] => 0        -- Empty sequence, residue is 0.
   249‚Üí  | 0 :: s => 1 + s.length -- If the largest degree is 0 (and the list is sorted), all are 0.
   250‚Üí  | s => residueAux (havelHakimiStep s) -- Apply one reduction step and recurse.
   251‚Üí
   252‚Üí/--
   253‚ÜíComputes the residue of a graph G, ,i.e. the number of zeros remaining after iteratively applying the Havel-Hakimi
   254‚Üíalgorithm to the degree sequence until all remaining degrees are zero.
   255‚ÜíStarts with the descending degree sequence and applies the Havel-Hakimi process.
   256‚Üí-/
   257‚Üínoncomputable def residue (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   258‚Üí  -- Get the degree sequence sorted in descending order and apply `residueAux`.
   259‚Üí  residueAux ((Finset.univ.image fun v => G.degree v).sort (¬∑ ‚Č• ¬∑))
   260‚Üí
   261‚Üí/--
   262‚ÜíFractional alpha. This is defined as
   263‚Üí$$
   264‚Üí\max_x \sum_{v \in V} x_v
   265‚Üí$$
   266‚Üísubject to $0 \le x_v \le 1$ for all $v \in V$ and
   267‚Üí$x_u + x_v \le 1$ whenever $(u, v)$ are connected by an edge.
   268‚Üí-/
   269‚Üínoncomputable def fractionalAlpha (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
   270‚Üí  sSup {(‚ąĎ i, x i) | (x : őĪ ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (_ : ‚ąÄ v, x v ‚ąą Set.Icc 0 1)
   271‚Üí    (_ : ‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí x u + x v ‚Č§ 1)}
   272‚Üí
   273‚Üí/--
   274‚ÜíLov√°sz Theta Function (ŌĎ(G))
   275‚ÜíThe Lov√°sz theta function is defined as:
   276‚ÜíŌĎ(G) = min őĽ_max(A)
   277‚Üíwhere the minimum is taken over all symmetric matrices A such that:
   278‚Üí
   279‚ÜíA_ij = 1 for all i = j (diagonal entries are 1)
   280‚ÜíA_ij = 0 for all {i,j} ‚ąą E (entries corresponding to edges are 0)
   281‚ÜíA is positive semidefinite
   282‚Üí
   283‚ÜíHere őĽ_max(A) denotes the maximum eigenvalue of A.
   284‚Üí-/
   285‚Üínoncomputable def lovaczThetaFunction
   286‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚ĄĚ :=
   287‚Üí  sInf {(Matrix.IsHermitian.maxEigenvalue hA) | (A : Matrix őĪ őĪ ‚ĄĚ) (hA : A.IsHermitian)
   288‚Üí      (_ : ‚ąÄ i, A i i = 1) (_ : ‚ąÄ i j, G.Adj i j ‚Üí A i j = 0)}
   289‚Üí
   290‚Üí/--
   291‚ÜíGiven a simple graph `G` with adjacency matrix `A`, this is the number `n‚āÄ + min n‚āä n‚āč` where:
   292‚Üí- `n‚āÄ` is the multiplicity of zero as an eigenvalue of `A`
   293‚Üí- `n‚āä` is the number of positive eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   294‚Üí- `n‚āč` is the number of negative eigenvalues of `A` (counting multiplicities)
   295‚Üí-/
   296‚Üínoncomputable def cvetkovic (G : SimpleGraph őĪ) [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   297‚Üí  letI A : Matrix őĪ őĪ ‚ĄĚ := G.adjMatrix ‚ĄĚ
   298‚Üí  letI spectrum : Multiset ‚ĄĚ := (Matrix.charpoly A).roots
   299‚Üí  letI positive_count : ‚Ąē := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 < x)
   300‚Üí  letI negative_count : ‚Ąē := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 > x)
   301‚Üí  letI zero_count : ‚Ąē := spectrum.countP (fun x => 0 = x)
   302‚Üí  zero_count + min positive_count negative_count
   303‚Üí
   304‚Üíend SimpleGraph
   305‚Üí

   353‚Üí  intro v

   356‚Üí/-- The minimum degree of all vertices (and `0` if there are no vertices).
   357‚ÜíThe key properties of this are given in `exists_minimal_degree_vertex`, `minDegree_le_degree`
   358‚Üíand `le_minDegree_of_forall_le_degree`. -/
   359‚Üídef minDegree [DecidableRel G.Adj] : ‚Ąē :=
   360‚Üí  WithTop.untopD 0 (univ.image fun v => G.degree v).min
   361‚Üí
   362‚Üí/-- There exists a vertex of minimal degree. Note the assumption of being nonempty is necessary, as
   363‚Üíthe lemma implies there exists a vertex. -/
   364‚Üítheorem exists_minimal_degree_vertex [DecidableRel G.Adj] [Nonempty V] :
   365‚Üí    ‚ąÉ v, G.minDegree = G.degree v := by
   366‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®t, ht : _ = _‚ü© := min_of_nonempty (univ_nonempty.image fun v => G.degree v)
   367‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®v, _, rfl‚ü© := mem_image.mp (mem_of_min ht)
   368‚Üí  exact ‚ü®v, by simp [minDegree, ht]‚ü©
   369‚Üí
   370‚Üí/-- The minimum degree in the graph is at most the degree of any particular vertex. -/
   371‚Üítheorem minDegree_le_degree [DecidableRel G.Adj] (v : V) : G.minDegree ‚Č§ G.degree v := by
   372‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®t, ht‚ü© := Finset.min_of_mem (mem_image_of_mem (fun v => G.degree v) (mem_univ v))
   373‚Üí  have := Finset.min_le_of_eq (mem_image_of_mem _ (mem_univ v)) ht
   374‚Üí  rwa [minDegree, ht]
   375‚Üí
   376‚Üí/-- In a nonempty graph, if `k` is at most the degree of every vertex, it is at most the minimum
   377‚Üídegree. Note the assumption that the graph is nonempty is necessary as long as `G.minDegree` is
   378‚Üídefined to be a natural. -/
   379‚Üítheorem le_minDegree_of_forall_le_degree [DecidableRel G.Adj] [Nonempty V] (k : ‚Ąē)
   380‚Üí    (h : ‚ąÄ v, k ‚Č§ G.degree v) : k ‚Č§ G.minDegree := by
   381‚Üí  rcases G.exists_minimal_degree_vertex with ‚ü®v, hv‚ü©
   382‚Üí  rw [hv]
   383‚Üí  apply h
